# Lore

> Lore is an open-source personal database for the AI era. Data lives in local SQLite, AI agents handle structuring, and every change is versioned like Git. No schema design, no migrations, no cloud required.

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://getlore.sh/docs/getting-started): Install Lore and create your first AI-managed database.
- [How It Works](https://getlore.sh/docs/how-it-works): The two-layer architecture that separates AI planning from database execution.
- [CLI Reference](https://getlore.sh/docs/cli-reference): Complete reference for all commands, flags, and options.
- [Operation Plans](https://getlore.sh/docs/operation-plans): The JSON plan format, all operation types, and how schema and data operations work.
- [Versioning](https://getlore.sh/docs/versioning): Commits, effect tracking, history, and revert.
- [Integrity](https://getlore.sh/docs/integrity): Verify the hash chain, recover from snapshots, and ensure data integrity.
- [Remote Sync](https://getlore.sh/docs/remote-sync): Sync with Turso or LibSQL remotes using push, pull, and clone.
- [AI Integration](https://getlore.sh/docs/ai-integration): How skill files teach AI agents to manage your database.
- [Full Documentation](https://getlore.sh/llms-full.txt): All documentation in a single file.

## Optional

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/lore-sh/lore): Source code for CLI, core engine, studio UI, and website.
- [Install Script](https://getlore.sh/install): Shell installer script.
